setting.note: Three generations of a Greek American family, told by Cal Stephanides, run from a burning city on the Aegean to the assembly lines and streets of Detroit. The map holds the east-side Greek quarter where the grandparents land, the boulevard where the city caught fire in 1967, and the quiet modernist house out in Grosse Pointe where the family finally lands.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Jeffrey Eugenides, first published 2002. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Middlesex, Jeffrey Eugenides (2002). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A rust and brick red for the Motor City and the fires that bracket the saga, from the Smyrna quay to Twelfth Street. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
